chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constt. Petition No. D –26 of 2023. Petitioner: Sajid Ali Ghunio through Yaseen Ali Ghunio, Advocate. Respondents: P.O Sindh and others. Date of Hearing: 07.12.2023. ORDER YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, J - The petitioner has invoked the jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of the Constitution, paradoxically seeking directions that his appointment in BPS-01 be declared as being against the law, while contending that such appointment ought to have been made between BPS-05 to 11 as his deceased father was serving as a Food Inspector in BPS-07, and with it being alleged that other persons who had similarly sought their appointments on the basis of deceased quota have been appointed to posts of a higher grade than that of the Petitioner. As such, it has been sought that the respondents be directed to upgrade him accordingly. Be that as it may, on query posed to learned counsel for the Petitioner as to whether the Petitioner had accepted the offer made him for his appointment as Chowkidar in BPS-01, he conceded that it was so. Furthermore, when called upon to demonstrate the legal right of the petitioner to upgradation, as sought, no cogent response was forthcoming. Under the circumstances, the petition is found to be misconceived and stands dismissed accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE Akber.
